summary: This cluster focuses on the genetic manipulation of the red alga *Porphyra tenera*, specifically through the characterization and application of its natural plasmid, PT4. Researchers isolated and sequenced this 1808-bp circular DNA molecule, identifying inverted repeat elements and a potential protein-coding region, which they used to construct a transformation vector. This vector successfully expressed green fluorescent protein (GFP), demonstrating a functional system for transgenic cell development. The work establishes a foundational method for selective breeding and genetic engineering in red algae, with direct implications for aquaculture and marine biotechnology.
